info.title: Kirim.Email SMTP API Documentation
info.description: Kirim.Email SMTP Domain and credential management API Documentation

get.description: Retrieve SMTP credentials for the authenticated domain using domain API key authentication

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain name for API key authentication. Must match the domain associated with the provided API key.

success.description: Indicates if the request was successful

data.description: Array of credential objects for the authenticated domain with pagination metadata

post.description: Create a new SMTP credential for the authenticated domain using domain API key authentication

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain name for API key authentication. Must match the domain associated with the provided API key.

requestBody.description: SMTP credential creation details for domain API authentication

success.description: Indicates if the request was successful

password.description: Generated password for the credential. Store this securely as it cannot be retrieved later

remote_synced.description: Whether the credential was successfully synchronized with the remote SMTP server

get.summary: Get credential details for authenticated domain
get.description: Retrieve details of a specific SMTP credential for the authenticated domain using domain API key authentication

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain name for API key authentication. Must match the domain associated with the provided API key.

success.description: Indicates if the request was successful

delete.description: Delete a specific SMTP credential for the authenticated domain using domain API key authentication

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain name for API key authentication. Must match the domain associated with the provided API key.

success.description: Indicates if the request was successful

message.description: Human-readable success message confirming the deletion

post.summary: Reset credential password for authenticated domain
post.description: Reset the password for a specific SMTP credential for the authenticated domain using domain API key authentication

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain name for API key authentication. Must match the domain associated with the provided API key.

success.description: Indicates if the request was successful

new_password.description: The new generated password. Store this securely as it cannot be retrieved later

strength_info.description: Password strength analysis and complexity information

remote_synced.description: Whether the password change was successfully synchronized with the remote SMTP server

get.description: Retrieve logs for authenticated domain using domain API key authentication

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain name for API key authentication. Must match the domain associated with the provided API key.

200.description: Successfully retrieved email logs for authenticated domain with optional filtering and pagination

data.description: Array of email log entries for the specified domain

items.description: Email log entry containing delivery and tracking information

id.description: Unique identifier for the log entry

user_guid.description: GUID of the user who sent the email

user_domain_guid.description: GUID of the domain used to send the email

event_type.description: Type of email event (sent, delivered, bounced, opened, clicked, etc.)

message_guid.description: Unique identifier for the email message

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp when the event occurred

count.description: Number of log entries returned in this response

offset.description: Number of entries skipped from the beginning

limit.description: Maximum number of entries requested per page

pagination.description: Pagination metadata for the log entries

total.description: Total number of log entries available

get.summary: Get logs for specific message in authenticated domain
get.description: Retrieve all logs for a specific message in authenticated domain using domain API key authentication

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain name for API key authentication. Must match the domain associated with the provided API key.

data.description: Array of log entries for the specific message

items.description: Email log entry for the specific message

id.description: Unique identifier for the log entry

user_guid.description: GUID of the user who sent the email

user_domain_guid.description: GUID of the domain used to send the email

event_type.description: Type of email event (sent, delivered, bounced, opened, clicked, etc.)

message_guid.description: Unique identifier for the email message

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp when the event occurred

get.summary: Stream logs for authenticated domain in real-time
get.description: Stream email logs in real-time using Server-Sent Events for authenticated domain

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain name for API key authentication. Must match the domain associated with the provided API key.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of logs to stream

schema.description: SSE stream with log data batches and completion events

post.summary: Send transactional email via domain API key with optional attachments
post.description: Send a transactional email using domain API key authentication. This endpoint supports bulk sending to multiple recipients, file attachments with advanced processing options, and comprehensive email customization. The email processing is handled asynchronously via job queues for optimal performance.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain name for API key authentication. Must match the 'from' email address domain.

requestBody.description: Email content and configuration. Supports both single recipient and bulk sending.

from.description: Sender email address. Must belong to the authenticated domain. Used for envelope sender and Return-Path header.

from_name.description: Optional sender display name. If not provided, will use the email address only.

to.description: Recipient email address(es). Single email string or array of emails for bulk sending. Maximum 1000 recipients per request.

subject.description: Email subject line. Supports Unicode characters and emojis.

text.description: Required plain text content for the email body. This will be used as fallback for email clients that don't support HTML. Line breaks will be preserved.
text.example:
Dear Customer,

We are writing to inform you about an important update to your account.

Best regards,
The Team

html.description: Optional HTML content for the email body. If provided, this will be used for HTML email clients. Should be a complete HTML document or HTML fragment.
html.example:
Important Account Update

Dear Customer,

We are writing to inform you about an important update to your account.

Best regards,
The Team

headers.description: Optional custom email headers. Can be provided as an object or JSON string. Common use cases include tracking headers, priority settings, and unsubscribe links.

reply_to.description: Reply-to email address. Must share the same top-level domain as the 'from' address for security compliance.

attachments.description: File attachment(s). Supports single file or array of files. Maximum 10 files per email, 20MB per file, 25MB total payload. Supported formats: PDF, DOC, DOCX, XLS, XLSX, PPT, PPTX, TXT, CSV, JPG, JPEG, PNG, GIF, WEBP, ZIP, RAR, 7Z.

attachment_options.description: JSON string with advanced attachment processing options. Available options: compression, password protection, and PDF watermarking.

202.description: Email send jobs successfully dispatched for asynchronous processing

message.description: Detailed success message with recipient and job count information
message.example: Email send jobs dispatched for 3 recipients in 1 job(s) with 2 attachment(s)

post.summary: Send transactional email using pre-defined template with variable replacement
post.description: Send emails using templates created in the platform. Supports variable replacement using {{variable}} syntax in both subject and content. Template must belong to the authenticated domain. Supports attachments with the same processing options as non-template message endpoint.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Domain name for API key authentication. Must match the domain associated with the provided API key.

template_guid.description: UUID of the template to use

to.description: Recipient email address(es). Single email string or array of emails for bulk sending. Maximum 1000 recipients per request.

from.description: Sender email address. Must belong to the authenticated domain. Defaults to noreply@{domain} if not provided.

from_name.description: Optional sender display name. If not provided, will use the email address only.

variables.description: Variables for template replacement using {{variable}} syntax. Can be single object for all recipients or array matching recipient count

oneOf.description: Single set of variables for all recipients
Array of variable objects, one per recipient

headers.description: Optional custom email headers. Can be provided as an object or JSON string. Common use cases include tracking headers, priority settings, and unsubscribe links.

reply_to.description: Reply-to email address. Must share the same top-level domain as the 'from' address for security compliance.

template_guid.description: UUID of the template to use

to.description: Recipient email address(es). Single email string or array of emails for bulk sending. Maximum 1000 recipients per request.

from.description: Sender email address. Must belong to the authenticated domain. Defaults to noreply@{domain} if not provided.

from_name.description: Optional sender display name. If not provided, will use the email address only.

variables.description: JSON string of variables for template replacement. Can be single object for all recipients or array matching recipient count

headers.description: JSON string with custom email headers. Common use cases include tracking headers, priority settings, and unsubscribe links.

reply_to.description: Reply-to email address. Must share the same top-level domain as the 'from' address for security compliance.

attachments.description: File attachment(s). Single file or array of files. Maximum 10 files per email, 20MB per file, 25MB total payload. Supports documents, images, and archives with optional processing.

attachment_options.description: JSON string with attachment processing options. Watermark text is limited to 50 characters.

message.example: Template-based email jobs dispatched for 2 recipients in 1 job(s)

Credential.description: SMTP credential object containing authentication details for email sending

user_smtp_guid.description: Unique credential GUID used for API operations

username.description: SMTP username for authentication. Must be unique within the domain

created_at.description: Unix timestamp when the credential was created

modified_at.description: Unix timestamp when the credential was last modified

DomainAuthCredentialBadRequest.description: Bad Request - Domain data missing or invalid

DomainAuthCredentialUnauthorized.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key authentication

DomainAuthCredentialForbidden.description: Forbidden - Credential does not belong to the authenticated domain

message.example: Credential does not belong to the authenticated domain
An error occurred while processing the request.

DomainAuthCredentialTooManyRequests.description: Too Many Requests - Rate limit exceeded. Retry after the specified time.

message.example: Too many attempts. Please try again later.

DomainAuthLogBadRequest.description: Bad Request - Domain data missing or invalid

DomainAuthLogUnauthorized.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key authentication

DomainAuthLogNotFound.description: Not Found - Domain or log not found

DomainAuthLogTooManyRequests.description: Too Many Requests - Rate limit exceeded. Retry after the specified time.

message.example: Too many attempts. Please try again later.

DomainAuthMessageBadRequest.description: Bad Request - Domain data missing or invalid request

DomainAuthMessageUnauthorized.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

DomainAuthMessageForbidden.description: Forbidden - Domain access denied or insufficient permissions

DomainAuthMessageNotFound.description: Domain not found or not configured

DomainAuthMessageValidationError.description: Validation Error - Invalid input data or business rule violation

error.example: File size exceeds maximum allowed size

DomainAuthMessageServerError.description: Internal Server Error - Unexpected system error

message.example: An error occurred while sending the message. Please try again.

DomainAuthMessageTooManyRequests.description: Too Many Requests - Rate limit exceeded. Retry after the specified time.

message.example: Too many attempts. Please try again later.

DomainAuthTemplateBadRequest.description: Bad Request - Domain data missing or invalid request

DomainAuthTemplateUnauthorized.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key authentication

DomainAuthTemplateNotFound.description: Not Found - Domain or template not found

message.example: An error occurred while sending the template message. Please try again.

DomainAuthTemplateTooManyRequests.description: Too Many Requests - Rate limit exceeded. Retry after the specified time.

message.example: Too many attempts. Please try again later.

DomainAuthSuppressionBadRequest.description: Bad Request - Domain data missing or invalid

DomainAuthSuppressionUnauthorized.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key authentication

message.example: An error occurred while processing the request.

DomainAuthSuppressionTooManyRequests.description: Too Many Requests - Rate limit exceeded. Retry after the specified time.

message.example: Too many attempts. Please try again later.

domainAuth.description: Domain API Key Authentication - Basic auth with API key:secret from domain configuration, requires 'domain' header
